pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates this file at this exact path
export_result.sh expects output at this path
export_result.sh expects PNG export at this path

All labels verified against specification in setup_task.sh and cross-referenced with 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., 2017). Each term appears in the transformer specification: Multi-Head Attention (encoder/decoder sublayers), Masked (Masked Multi-Head Attention in decoder), Feed Forward (encoder/decoder sublayers), Add & Norm (residual connections with layer normalization), Linear (final layer), Softmax (output layer), Positional Encoding (added to embeddings in both encoder/decoder), Embedding (Input Embedding and Output Embedding)

Based on transformer architecture: encoder has 2 sub-layers (Multi-Head Attention, Feed Forward) each with Add & Norm = 2 blocks; decoder has 3 sub-layers (Masked Attention, Attention, Feed Forward) each with Add & Norm = 3 blocks. Total for one Nx iteration: 2 + 3 = 5. This matches the required minimum.

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a Transformer architecture diagram based on the 'Attention Is All You Need' paper (Vaswani et al., 2017). The specification file, created by setup_task.sh, accurately describes the encoder-decoder architecture with: Input/Output Embeddings, Positional Encoding layers, Multi-Head Attention in both stacks, Masked Multi-Head Attention in decoder only, Feed Forward networks in both stacks, residual connections represented as Add & Norm blocks (2 in encoder, 3 in decoder per Nx iteration totaling 5 minimum), and final Linear/Softmax layers for output. All required labels are correctly specified in the metadata and match the authoritative transformer architecture. Output files are saved to /home/ga/Desktop/ as .drawio and .png formats.
